title: The One with Chandler's Work Laugh

jokes.text: Sorry. Monica and Chandler are "making love."
jokes.setup: Rachel objects to Joey's crude phrasing about Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey's overly formal correction misses the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - earnest attempt at politeness while completely missing Rachel's actual concern. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: They did it right there on the couch.
jokes.setup: Rachel peppers Joey with questions about Monica/Chandler details, Joey claims ignorance
jokes.punchline: Joey suddenly volunteers the most awkward detail possible
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - Joey's repeated 'I don't know' builds expectation, then he drops the most uncomfortable detail. Strong impact.

jokes.text: Someone I know is getting married. Yeah, weddings are happy occasions. Oh, by the by, it's my ex-wife, Emily!
jokes.setup: Ross starts cheerfully talking about weddings being happy
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that it's his ex-wife remarrying
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross misdirection - starts positive then reveals devastating news with forced cheerfulness. Good character work.

jokes.text: Stupid British snack food!
jokes.setup: Ross is clearly upset about Emily's remarriage
jokes.punchline: Takes out anger on innocent scone
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment showing Ross's displaced anger, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Did they teach you that in anger management?
jokes.setup: Ross's outburst at the scone
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's deadpan observation about anger management
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe timing and callback to established character history. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Are you still mad about, you know, the Louisiana Purchase?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's advice about not being mad about the past
jokes.punchline: Bizarre historical reference as example
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdism - completely random historical reference that somehow makes sense in her logic. Original and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Anybody going to eat that?
jokes.setup: Ross just destroyed his scone in anger
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely practical concern about wasted food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely oblivious to emotional context, only cares about food. Maximum economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, Bing! Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Doug arrives at the office party
jokes.punchline: Overly familiar, loud greeting to subordinate
jokes.explanation: Sets up the obnoxious boss character well but fairly standard workplace comedy trope.

jokes.text: Ha-ha-ha! I ask myself that very question. This is Monica.
jokes.setup: Doug asks who the pretty lady is and what she's doing with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chandler's insecurity and willingness to put himself down for his boss.

jokes.text: Dewey, Cheatem & Howe
jokes.setup: Doug mentions a new law firm
jokes.punchline: Classic fake law firm name pun
jokes.explanation: Extremely old and overused joke. Shows Doug's hacky sense of humor but lacks originality.

jokes.text: What was that? That noise you made? Oh, that was my work laugh.
jokes.setup: Chandler laughs loudly at Doug's bad joke
jokes.punchline: Monica's confusion leads to revelation of 'work laugh'
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about workplace fakeness. 'Work laugh' becomes a quotable concept that many can relate to.

jokes.text: He says, '$30, Father. Same as in town.' Ha!
jokes.setup: Monica tries to create her own work laugh
jokes.punchline: Delivers inappropriate prostitution joke at office party
jokes.explanation: Monica's complete misunderstanding of appropriate work humor creates perfect cringe comedy. Shows her social awkwardness.

jokes.text: Except for that time at that strip club. Strip church.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions Doug never talks outside work except strip club
jokes.punchline: Awkward cover-up attempt with 'strip church'
jokes.explanation: Chandler's panic cover-up is perfectly in character. 'Strip church' is absurd enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: I used to, but then Joey thought it'd be fun to go to Central Park and hit rocks at bigger rocks.
jokes.setup: Monica asks about tennis racket
jokes.punchline: Joey's destructive 'game' ruined the equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Joey's childlike destructiveness. The image of hitting rocks at bigger rocks is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Okay, good luck with that.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions she lent her racket to Joey and never got it back
jokes.punchline: Rachel's resigned acceptance of Joey's track record
jokes.explanation: Nice understated reaction showing everyone knows Joey's habits. Economical but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: If there was, I wouldn't tell you.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Rachel has something to talk about
jokes.punchline: Rachel's honest admission she wouldn't confide anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the tension in their friendship. Rachel's blunt honesty is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: Duck quacking and rooster clucking conversation
jokes.setup: Phoebe appears to be having a serious conversation
jokes.punchline: She's actually talking to the duck and rooster like a therapist
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe absurdism - treating farm animals like therapy patients. Highly original visual gag perfectly suited to her character.

jokes.text: Tom Hanks, Meg Ryan. They get mail and stuff.
jokes.setup: Joey tries to convince Ross to come to the movies
jokes.punchline: Joey's oversimplified description of romantic comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - reduces complex romantic plot to 'they get mail and stuff.' Shows his simple perspective on relationships.

jokes.text: Those birds are browned, basted and ready to be carved.
jokes.setup: Monica dominates at tennis
jokes.punchline: Cooking metaphor for defeating opponents
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature expressed through her chef vocabulary. Good character integration but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Okay, easy, Martina.
jokes.setup: Monica's intense tennis competitiveness
jokes.punchline: Reference to Martina Navratilova
jokes.explanation: Decent sports reference to famous tennis player. Shows Chandler trying to reign in Monica's intensity.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I don't understand what you just said.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggests letting the boss win
jokes.punchline: Monica's complete inability to comprehend losing on purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - her competitive nature makes the concept of losing impossible to understand. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: He can't even breathe, and she's popping pills.
jokes.setup: Monica describes their opponents' condition
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about their physical distress
jokes.explanation: Monica's ruthless competitive assessment of opponents' weaknesses. Dark humor about their suffering.

jokes.text: Oopsie! Missed it!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to throw the game
jokes.punchline: Fake innocent reaction to deliberate miss
jokes.explanation: Monica's terrible acting while trying to lose. Shows her inability to be convincingly bad at sports.

jokes.text: Not if I see you first, Doug!
jokes.setup: Chandler's work personality being criticized
jokes.punchline: Example of his cringey workplace banter
jokes.explanation: Good example of workplace fake friendliness. Cringey but relatable to anyone who's had to schmooze at work.

jokes.text: You know what? Because you said that... I'm not putting out tonight!
jokes.setup: Monica criticizes Chandler's work behavior
jokes.punchline: Chandler threatens to withhold sex like a stereotypical wife
jokes.explanation: Gender role reversal joke. Shows Chandler's petulant side and relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: Ross couldn't fit down the trash chute. That's right. He almost could. Which is exactly how I got stuck there.
jokes.setup: Discussing where Ross might be
jokes.punchline: Chandler's experience getting stuck in trash chute
jokes.explanation: Good callback to previous physical comedy. Reveals character backstory while explaining current logic.

jokes.text: Oh, out! God, I don't know why we didn't think to check there.
jokes.setup: Ross vaguely says he was 'out' all night
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic response to the non-answer
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback to Ross's evasiveness. Shows Rachel's wit in dealing with annoying behavior.

jokes.text: He hooked up!
jokes.setup: Ross admits to walking around the city alone all night
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately deduces the real reason
jokes.explanation: Joey's street smarts about hookup culture. Maximum economy - two words that change the entire conversation.

jokes.text: Janice's nasal laugh after returning Ross's scarf
jokes.setup: Mystery woman returns Ross's scarf
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be Janice through her distinctive laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal structure - audience figures it out from the laugh before seeing her. Maximum impact through recognition.

jokes.text: I know what you all are thinking, but Chandler is in Yemen.
jokes.setup: Janice anticipating judgment about being with Ross
jokes.punchline: Reference to Chandler's elaborate escape to Yemen
jokes.explanation: Good callback to major plot point. Shows Janice's awareness of why Chandler left while justifying her actions.

jokes.text: You two would have very hairy children.
jokes.setup: Ross announces he's going to see Janice again
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt observation about genetic implications
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - completely inappropriate but hilariously accurate observation. Unexpected angle on the relationship that's both crude and logical.

jokes.text: In high school, I made out with James even though I knew that you liked him.
jokes.setup: Rachel says she has something to confess she's never admitted
jokes.punchline: Random high school betrayal instead of Monica/Chandler knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation for Monica/Chandler confession but delivers unrelated guilt. Awkward timing.

jokes.text: You know what your nickname is, Mr. Big.
jokes.setup: Rachel recounts overheard phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Awkward intimate nickname revelation
jokes.explanation: Moderately effective cringe moment but the nickname feels generic. Creates awkwardness without being particularly clever.

jokes.text: Mr. Bigot. Ha, ha. He tells the most racist jokes.
jokes.setup: Monica needs to explain away 'Mr. Big' nickname
jokes.punchline: Desperate cover story that makes Chandler sound terrible
jokes.explanation: Monica's panic leads to making Chandler sound worse than if she'd just admitted the relationship. Good escalation of lies.

jokes.text: Monica's exaggerated laugh at 'Me and Chandler?'
jokes.setup: Rachel directly asks about Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic fake laugh of denial
jokes.explanation: Classic 'lady doth protest too much' moment. Monica's terrible acting makes the denial more suspicious.

jokes.text: That's not a Nutter Butter. That's an old won ton! Judge rules... Nutter Butter.
jokes.setup: Duck wins the treat-finding contest
jokes.punchline: Joey's elaborate judging of duck vs rooster game with wrong food identification
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd - treating pet competition like official sporting event. Joey's serious judging of nonsense is perfect character work.

jokes.text: We should all support President Clinton and her husband, Bill.
jokes.setup: Doug making political joke
jokes.punchline: Reversing Hillary and Bill Clinton roles
jokes.explanation: Dated political joke that wasn't particularly clever even when current. Shows Doug's hacky humor sense.

jokes.text: I'll have Monica stick her finger in it. That ought to sweeten it up.
jokes.setup: Doug asks how they like their coffee
jokes.punchline: Chandler's romantic compliment disguised as coffee joke
jokes.explanation: Chandler finding a way to be romantic while maintaining his work persona. Clever dual meaning that works on both levels.

jokes.text: Kara's coffee is weak-tasting. But what Doug did was imply that it was weak physically.
jokes.setup: Doug's bad coffee joke falls flat
jokes.punchline: Monica explaining the joke like teaching a child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about explaining jokes. Monica's condescending tone while saving Chandler's job is peak cringe comedy.

jokes.text: She keeps lying to me. That's it! I'm gonna go over there and I'm confronting them right now.
jokes.setup: Rachel discovers Monica lied about working late
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic decision to confront them
jokes.explanation: Standard plot advancement moment. Shows Rachel's frustration but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: I actually came over here to borrow this lamp... to, um, look at my books. You know, see them a little better.
jokes.setup: Rachel caught at Monica and Chandler's place unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Terrible improvised excuse involving lamp for reading
jokes.explanation: Rachel's bad lying creates awkward moment. The lamp excuse is absurd enough to be uncomfortable.

jokes.text: What a great way to make some extra pocket money.
jokes.setup: Monica claims Chandler pays her to clean
jokes.punchline: Rachel's fake enthusiasm for the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Rachel playing along with obvious lie while building internal case. Shows her detective work in progress.

jokes.text: Man, she is really gullible.
jokes.setup: Monica thinks she fooled Rachel with cleaning story
jokes.punchline: Monica's complete misreading of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - audience knows Rachel is onto them while Monica feels clever. Good setup for future reveals.

jokes.text: I believe that the sun has set on our day in the sun.
jokes.setup: Janice trying to break up with Ross gently
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic metaphor for ending relationship
jokes.explanation: Janice trying to be profound and poetic is funny because it's so unlike her usual bluntness. Verbose breakup line.

jokes.text: I just don't think I can take another second of you whining!
jokes.setup: Janice's poetic buildup to breakup
jokes.punchline: Blunt truth about Ross being too whiny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Janice, the most annoying character, finds ROSS too annoying. Perfect character reversal and harsh truth.

jokes.text: So you're saying I've become so whiny that I annoy you... Janice.
jokes.setup: Ross processing Janice's criticism
jokes.punchline: Ross's dawning realization of how bad he must be
jokes.explanation: Ross's slow realization that if he annoys JANICE, he must really be bad. Good character development through humor.

jokes.text: I guess, that's two out of three... Joey!
jokes.setup: Janice leaving Ross, seeing Joey arrive
jokes.punchline: Janice moving on to Joey as third option
jokes.explanation: Janice's methodical approach to dating the friends. Shows her pragmatic attitude and Joey's bad timing.

jokes.text: You don't fool around with your friends' ex-girlfriends or possible girlfriends or girls they're related to.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining rules about dating friends' exes
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and comprehensive list of forbidden women
jokes.explanation: Joey's surprisingly comprehensive understanding of dating ethics. The escalating list shows his experience with these situations.

jokes.text: I gave you 27 dollars. No strings attached!
jokes.setup: Ross listing reasons Joey should remember his generosity
jokes.punchline: Specific small amount and contradictory claim about no strings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - the oddly specific amount and immediate contradiction of 'no strings' while clearly attaching strings. Great escalation.

tmdb_overview: Monica discovers Chandler's annoying workplace personality; Ross dates Janice after learning Emily is remarrying.
